story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that tensions involving Iran have disrupted regional stability and global supply chains, with Iran’s actions prompting concerns over fuel shortages and rising energy costs. Mediators, including Doha, are actively working to de-escalate the situation, while the UAE’s involvement in an attack on Iran raises fears of broader conflict in the Gulf. Additionally, economic impacts are evident, as U.S. consumer price inflation reached 3.8% in April, partly driven by disruptions linked to the Iran war.

articles.title: Education Ministry gives fresh push for implementation of PM-SHRI in Bengal, Kerala, and T.N. post elections
articles.summary: The Union Education Ministry has urged the governments of West Bengal, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu to accelerate the implementation of the PM-SHRI scheme, a five-year initiative launched in September 2022. The scheme aims to upgrade over 14,500 schools into model institutions aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. The Ministry highlighted the limited time remaining for the scheme to complete its five-year duration and called for urgent action from the states.
articles.raw_summary: Launched in September 2022, this five-year initiative aims to transform over 14,500 schools into “exemplar institutions” that lead the way in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; only a limited time is left for the scheme to complete its five-year duration, the Ministry stated in a letter issued to the States

articles.title: Event Report | University School of Law and Legal Studies, GGSIPU successfully organises De Novo Moot Court Competition 2026
articles.summary: The University School of Law and Legal Studies at Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University organized the De Novo Moot Court Competition 2026, focusing on consumer protection and product liability law. articles.title: IBC | Appeals Must Be Filed With Certified Copy Of Impugned Order, Mere E-Filing Not Enough: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that appeals filed with the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) must include a certified copy of the impugned order; mere e-filing without this document is considered an incompetent appeal that cannot be cured later. A bench led by Justice Sanjay Kumar and Justice K. Vinod Chandran set aside an NCLAT order that had condoned a 150-day delay in refiling an appeal due to this procedural lapse.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday (May 12) observed that an e-filing of an appeal before the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) without a certified copy of the impugned order is not a mere defective filing but a “wholly incompetent appeal” that cannot subsequently be cured. A bench of Justice Sanjay Kumar and Justice K. articles.summary: The Airports Economic Regulatory Authority (AERA) has approved a user development fee (UDF) for domestic passengers at Noida's upcoming airport, starting at ₹490 and rising gradually to ₹1,461 for international passengers by FY31. The fee will fund the greenfield facility's development. AERA is the regulatory body overseeing airport tariffs in India.
articles.raw_summary: Tariff for greenfield facility to rise gradually till FY31 with UDF up to ₹1,461 for departing international passengers

articles.title: Governor dissolves West Bengal assembly after completion of term
articles.summary: The West Bengal assembly was dissolved after completing its five-year term, as the current government under Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress was elected in May 2021. This is a routine constitutional process following the completion of the assembly's term. The dissolution paves the way for new elections to constitute the next legislative body.
articles.raw_summary: The current assembly was constituted in May 2021 after the Trinamool Congress, under the leadership of Mamata Banerjee, returned to power for the third consecutive term in the state.

The move drops the minimum wage criteria, which was a contentious issue in earlier drafts. articles.title: Baramulla Consumer Commission Holds Postal Department Liable For Loss Of Parcel Containing Pashmina Shawls Worth ₹60,000

The commission directed the postal authorities to compensate the complainant, Mehvish Ashraf, for the loss and mental agony. The ruling highlights deficiencies in postal service delivery and consumer protection enforcement.

articles.title: Big SC order for Greater Noida real estate: GNIDA cannot impose penalties for its own delays, lapses
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority (GNIDA) cannot impose penalties on developers for its own delays or lapses. This decision clears a legal obstacle for Earth Infrastructures Ltd to revive its stalled projects and provides relief to thousands of homebuyers awaiting possession of their homes. The ruling underscores accountability in real estate regulation and could influence future policy on housing delays and developer penalties.
articles.raw_summary: SC ruling is significant as it clears legal hurdle in reviving Earth Infrastructures Ltd projects and offers relief to thousands of homebuyers waiting for possession of their homes.

articles.title: S.167 B(2) IT Act | Fixed Share Given To Member Of 'Association Of Persons' Regardless Of Profit Will Be Taxed As Income : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that fixed shares given to members of an Association of Persons (AOP) from gross receipts, without bearing business expenses or losses, will be taxed as income and not exempt under profit-sharing rules. The judgment clarifies taxation under Section 86 read with Section 167B(2) of the Income Tax Act. This decision impacts how members of AOPs or Bodies of Individuals (BOIs) report income for tax purposes.
articles.raw_summary: In an important ruling on a tax law, the Supreme Court on Tuesday (May 12) held that a member receiving a fixed percentage of gross receipts from an Association of Persons (AOP), without bearing business expenses or losses, cannot claim such receipts as a "share of profit” to claim exemption from income tax. It is worthwhile to mention that taxation of members in an Association of Persons (AOP) or Body of Individuals (BOI) is governed by Section 86, read with Section 167B (2) of the Income Tax A

articles.title: 'Oral Dictation Not Final' : Supreme Court Slams Party Who Alleged Signed Order Favouring Adani Ports Was Not Same As Oral Order
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an order dictated in open court is not final until signed by the bench. Only a signed order is enforceable, as dictated drafts may be altered before finalization. The Court emphasized that corrections post-dictation must not materially change the order. The ruling clarifies the legal process for judicial orders in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday (May 12) held that an order/judgment dictated in an open court cannot be deemed as final, as it may be open to alteration or modification by the bench post-dictation. The Court said that only a signed order remains enforceable and binding. “The Court might dictate a draft to keep the facts fresh in the mind and the draft so dictated then becomes final only after signing, subject to corrections and alterations which do not amount to a material change in the order. articles.title: GSTN Introduces Automated Annexure-B Utility for GST Refunds
articles.summary: The GST Network (GSTN) has replaced manual Annexure-B filing with an automated JSON-based utility for GST refunds. The new system, linked to Form RFD-01, includes invoice-level checks, ITC classification, and automated validations to streamline refund processing. This change aims to reduce manual errors and improve efficiency in GST refund claims.

articles.title: ITAT Reduces Demonetization Cash Deposit Addition to 10% Due to Supporting Evidence
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot reduced a tax addition related to demonetization cash deposits from 100% to 10% after the assessee provided supporting evidence such as land records and cash flow statements. The tribunal's decision restricts the disputed tax liability to a fraction of the original amount, highlighting the importance of documentary proof in tax disputes. This ruling impacts tax assessment procedures for demonetization-related cases in India.

articles.title: ITAT Accepts Spam Folder Explanation as Reasonable Cause for Missing Tax Notice
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a taxpayer's explanation for missing a tax notice—because it landed in the spam folder—was a reasonable cause. The tribunal directed the appellate authority to adopt a liberal approach while considering additional evidence and granted the assessee another chance to support their exemption claim. The decision highlights the judiciary's role in interpreting procedural lapses in tax compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that the appellate authority should have adopted a liberal approach while considering additional evidence crucial to adjudication. This decision impacts tax and trade policies for quicklime in India.

articles.title: Gold, silver import duty raised to 15% from 6% as govt moves to curb imports, support rupee
articles.summary: India has significantly increased import tariffs on gold and silver to 15% from 6% to curb imports and protect foreign exchange reserves. articles.title: West Bengal tax body rejects Flipkart’s GST exemption model for delivery charges
articles.summary: The West Bengal Appellate Authority rejected Flipkart's attempt to exempt delivery charges from GST by classifying itself as a Goods Transport Agency (GTA). The ruling deemed Flipkart's model a 'legal fiction' and not a genuine GTA service, highlighting its organized logistics operations. This decision impacts Flipkart's tax liability and sets a precedent for e-commerce logistics taxation in India.

articles.title: 'People's will through lawmakers can bring religious reforms': Supreme Court
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned that constitutional courts should avoid altering religious practices, stating that only elected representatives can bring about reforms in religious customs. The court emphasized that judicial scrutiny of every religious practice is impractical. This ruling underscores the judiciary's reluctance to intervene in religious matters without legislative backing.
articles.raw_summary: Constitutional courts should be extremely reluctant to alter religious practices, a nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned. Only the will of the people, expressed through elected representatives in Parliament and assemblies, can bring about reforms in religious customs. The court emphasized that it's impossible to scrutinize every religious practice.

articles.title: Iraq, Pakistan strike energy deals with Iran as Tehran flexes Hormuz control
articles.summary: Iraq and Pakistan have signed agreements with Iran to ensure safe transit of oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) through the Strait of Hormuz. This comes as Iran tightens control over the strait amid supply disruptions caused by regional conflicts. The deals highlight the geopolitical and economic implications for energy-dependent nations like India.

articles.title: 11:11 | PM Modi's appeal to shield India’s economy from West Asia crisis; Market impact; New ITR rules & more
articles.summary: Good morning! In today’s edition of 11:11, we look at PM Narendra Modi's call for "collective responsibility," urging citizens to cut fuel and gold consumption, among other things, to shield India’s economy from West Asia’s oil crisis. Markets reacted swiftly, with EV stocks surging while jewellers and aviation dipped. Plus, the government fully operationalises new Labour Codes and revamps FY26 ITR forms for higher transparency.

It also lowered the 2027 growth outlook to 6 per cent from earlier estimates, pointing to lingering global and domestic pressures.

articles.title: UP cop alleges systemic corruption in Lucknow Police, appeals to CM Yogi for impartial inquiry
articles.summary: A Lucknow Police constable has publicly accused senior officers in the Lucknow Police Commissionerate of systemic corruption. In a video appeal to Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath, the constable alleged that the officers named in his complaints are overseeing the ongoing departmental inquiry, demanding instead an independent investigation. The constable questioned the credibility of the internal probe and sought external oversight to ensure fairness.
articles.raw_summary: A Lucknow Police constable has intensified his allegations of corruption within the commissionerate by releasing a second video appeal to Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath. The constable questioned the credibility of the ongoing departmental inquiry and demanded an investigation by an independent agency, claiming the officers named in his allegations are themselves supervising the probe.

articles.title: India Cannot Sell Its Cotton to the World Until It Admits Why the World Stopped Buying
articles.summary: India's cotton sector has a productivity problem, a quality problem and a market-credibility problem. On Tuesday, the Union Cabinet approved a Rs 5,659.22 crore mission that proposes to address all three simultaneously, through seeds, ginning modernisation, traceability infrastructure and a national branding programme. What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

The situation may influence India's energy policy and economic outlook.

The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments, and its closure could disrupt energy supplies worldwide.

articles.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
articles.summary: As the kharif season approaches, Indian farmers are considering growing soybean and pulses like urad and masur instead of paddy and maize. This shift is driven by concerns over erratic monsoons and potential fertilizer shortages. Maize planting is expected to decline as farmers opt for lower-risk, less fertilizer-dependent crops amid unpredictable weather conditions.
articles.raw_summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

articles.title: Iran just redrew the Strait of Hormuz. New IRGC map shows why the world is worried
articles.summary: Iran has released a new map by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) that redefines the boundaries of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments. The move raises concerns about potential disruptions to maritime traffic and energy supplies, given that nearly 20% of the world's oil passes through this strait. India, which relies heavily on oil imports from the Gulf region, could face indirect impacts on energy security and trade routes if tensions escalate.

articles.title: Delayed by a year due to tech glitches, livestock census to be out after June
articles.summary: The 21st Livestock Census report, which collects data on livestock and poultry populations in India, has been delayed by a year due to technical glitches in data compilation and validation. The census, conducted by the central government, concluded data collection in February 2023 but will now be released after June. The updated figures are crucial for informing government policies related to agriculture and food security.

This quinquennial exercise, which concluded its data collection in February last year, aims to provide crucial updated figures on livestock and poultry populations for policy decisions.

articles.title: Higher food prices drive April retail inflation to 3.5%
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India rose to 3.5% in April, primarily due to higher food prices. While fuel inflation slowed, restaurant services saw a significant price increase because of higher LPG costs. Consumer durables like cars and air conditioners became cheaper. Experts warn of potential upside risks to inflation despite the softer-than-expected reading.
articles.raw_summary: Retail inflation edged up to 3.5% in April, driven by a rise in food prices. While fuel inflation slowed, restaurant services saw a significant price jump due to higher LPG costs. Consumer durables like cars and air conditioners, however, became cheaper. Experts note upside risks to the inflation outlook despite the softer-than-expected reading.
